Full Directory Structure (apps/web) ``` apps/web/ ├── index.html # Root HTML entry point ├── public/ │ ├── favicon.ico │ ├── favicon-16x16.png │ ├── favicon-32x32.png │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png │ └── mockServiceWorker.js ├── src/ │ ├── main.tsx # Application entry point │ ├── index.css # Main stylesheet with Tailwind + theme │ ├── router.ts # TanStack Router configuration │ │ │ ├── routes/ # File-based routing │ │ ├── __root.tsx # Root layout with providers │ │ ├── _chat.tsx # Chat layout │ │ ├── _chat.index.tsx # Chat index │ │ ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx # Thread detail page │ │ ├── _chat.settings.tsx # Settings page │ │ └── routeTree.gen.ts # Generated route tree │ │ │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── ui/ # UI component library (shadcn/ui) │ │ │ ├── button.tsx │ │ │ ├── sidebar.tsx │ │ │ ├── dialog.tsx │ │ │ ├── toast.tsx │ │ │ ├── switch.tsx │ │ │ ├── select.tsx │ │ │ └── [40+ other UI components] │ │ │ │ │ ├── Sidebar.tsx │ │ ├── ChatView.tsx / ChatView.browser.tsx │ │ ├── ChatMarkdown.tsx │ │ ├── DiffPanel.tsx │ │ ├── DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx # Provider component │ │ ├── ThreadTerminalDrawer.tsx │ │ └── [other feature components] │ │ │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── useTheme.ts # Theme management hook │ │ ├── useMediaQuery.ts │ │ └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts │ │ │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── gitReactQuery.ts │ │ ├── providerReactQuery.ts │ │ ├── serverReactQuery.ts │ │ └── [utility libraries] │ │ │ ├── store.ts # Main Zustand store (AppState) │ ├── composerDraftStore.ts # Composer draft Zustand store │ ├── terminalStateStore.ts # Terminal state Zustand store │ ├── appSettings.ts # App settings management (localStorage) │ │ │ ├── types.ts # TypeScript type definitions │ ├── env.ts # Environment detection │ ├── branding.ts # Branding constants │ └── [utility files and logic modules] │ ├── components.json ├── package.json ├── tsconfig.json ├── vite.config.ts └── [config files] ``` --- ### 2. Main Entry Point (main.tsx) The application starts with `main.tsx` which: - Imports React and ReactDOM - Sets up routing with TanStack Router - Uses either HashHistory (Electron) or BrowserHistory (web) - Configures the router via `getRouter(history)` - Renders everything in a `` wrapped in `React.StrictMode` - Sets the document title from branding constants Entry point location: `/apps/web/index.html` (loads `src/main.tsx`) --- ### 3. React Context/Providers Architecture **No traditional React Context used.** Instead, the app uses: #### **Zustand Stores (3 main stores):** 1. **Main Store (`store.ts`):** - State: Projects, threads, hydration status - Uses localStorage key: `t3code:renderer-state:v8` - Exposes methods: `syncServerReadModel`, `markThreadVisited`, `toggleProject`, `setError`, etc. - Uses `persist` hook for localStorage sync 2. **Composer Draft Store (`composerDraftStore.ts`):** - State: Draft prompts, images, provider/model selections - localStorage key: `t3code:composer-drafts:v1` - Tracks draft threads and project-draft associations - Uses Zustand's `persist` middleware with `createJSONStorage` 3. **Terminal State Store (`terminalStateStore.ts`):** - State: Terminal UI state (open/closed, height, active terminal, groups) - localStorage key: `t3code:terminal-state:v1` - Persists only `terminalStateByThreadId` - Uses Zustand's `persist` middleware #### **Custom Hooks for External State:** - **`useTheme()` hook** (`hooks/useTheme.ts`): - Manual localStorage-based state management - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for reactive updates - Handles theme: "light" | "dark" | "system" - localStorage key: `t3code:theme` - Applies theme to `document.documentElement` with class toggling - Listens to system dark mode preference changes - **`useAppSettings()` hook** (`appSettings.ts`): - Uses Effect Schema for validation - localStorage key: `t3code:app-settings:v1` - Settings: codexBinaryPath, codexHomePath, customCodexModels, codexServiceTier, confirmThreadDelete, enableAssistantStreaming - Uses `useSyncExternalStore` for external state subscriptions - Storage event listener for cross-tab sync #### **Provider Components:** - **`` and ``** in `__root.tsx` - **``** in `_chat.tsx` - **``** from shadcn/ui in `_chat.tsx` - **``** (no-op provider in `store.ts` for persistence initialization) --- ### 4. CSS/Styling Architecture **Technology Stack:** - **Tailwind CSS v4** (with @theme support) - **CSS custom properties (variables)** for theming - **CSS animations** for skeleton loading **Main CSS File:** `/apps/web/src/index.css` **Key Features:** 1. **Theme System:** - Root CSS variables for light mode - Dark mode variant with `@variant dark` custom variant - Variables: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--secondary`, `--destructive`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, etc. - Light/Dark colors use oklch color space for primary 2. **Color Palette:** - Primary (oklch): Light 0.488, Dark 0.588 - Semantic colors: success, warning, info, destructive - Neutral grays: neutral-50 through neutral-950 - Opacity-based secondary colors 3. **Layout:** - Full viewport height for root elements - No scrollbars on body (overflow hidden) - Custom scrollbar styling with `-webkit-scrollbar` - Drag region support for frameless titlebar (`-webkit-app-region: drag`) 4. **Component Styling:** - No transitions during theme changes (`.no-transitions` class) - Terminal scrollbar parity styling - Chat markdown styling (extensive) - Diff panel viewport theming - Border radius system: `--radius` + variants (sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl, 3xl, 4xl) 5. **Typography:** - Font: "DM Sans" (Google Fonts) - Monospace: "SF Mono", SFMono-Regular, Consolas, Liberation Mono --- ### 5. Existing Configuration/Settings Components **Settings Route:** `/apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` **App Settings Management:** `/apps/web/src/appSettings.ts` **Settings Sections:** 1. **Appearance:** - Theme selector: System / Light / Dark - Resolved theme display 2. **Codex App Server:** - Codex binary path override - CODEX_HOME path configuration - Reset to defaults 3. **Models:** - Default service tier: Automatic / Fast / Flex - Custom model slugs per provider (Codex) - Add/remove custom models 4. **Responses:** - Stream assistant messages toggle - Restore defaults 5. **Keybindings:** - Config file path display - Open keybindings.json button 6. **Safety:** - Confirm thread deletion toggle - Restore defaults **Settings Data Structure:** ```typescript interface AppSettings { codexBinaryPath: string; codexHomePath: string; confirmThreadDelete: boolean; enableAssistantStreaming: boolean; codexServiceTier: "auto" | "fast" | "flex"; customCodexModels: string[]; } ``` --- ### 6. Root HTML File **Location:** `/apps/web/index.html` **Content:** ```html T3 Code (Alpha)
``` --- ### 7. LocalStorage Usage Throughout Codebase **LocalStorage Keys Used:** | Key | Scope | Data | Persistence | |-----|-------|------|-------------| | `t3code:renderer-state:v8` | App | Projects (expanded state), threads metadata | Main store | | `t3code:theme` | App | Theme preference ("light"\|"dark"\|"system") | useTheme hook | | `t3code:app-settings:v1` | App | Model paths, custom models, toggles | useAppSettings hook | | `t3code:composer-drafts:v1` | Thread | Draft prompts, attachments, model selections | Composer store | | `t3code:terminal-state:v1` | Thread | Terminal UI state, heights, group assignments | Terminal store | **Legacy Keys Cleaned Up:** - `t3code:renderer-state:v1-v7` - `codething:renderer-state:v1-v4` **Storage Patterns:** 1. **Zustand persist middleware:** - Used in `terminalStateStore` and `composerDraftStore` - Automatic sync on state changes - JSON serialization 2. **Manual useSyncExternalStore:** - Used in `useTheme` and `useAppSettings` - Custom subscribe/getSnapshot functions - StorageEvent listeners for cross-tab sync 3. **Store subscription pattern:** - `store.ts` uses `useStore.subscribe()` to persist on every state change - Manual `persistState()` function calls **Error Handling:** - All localStorage operations wrapped in try/catch - Quota errors ignored to avoid breaking UX - Falls back to default state on parse failures --- ## Summary The T3 Code monorepo web app uses a modern architecture combining: - **TanStack Router** for file-based routing - **Zustand** for state management (3 separate stores) - **Tailwind CSS v4** with CSS custom properties for theming - **shadcn/ui** for component library - **localStorage** for persistence (5 keys with versioning) - **Custom hooks** for theme and app settings using `useSyncExternalStore` - **No React Context providers** - entirely store-based - **Effect Schema** for app settings validation The app has a dedicated settings page with theme, model, binary path, and safety configurations. All state is persisted to localStorage with version keys to handle migrations.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: packages/shared & packages/contracts

---

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**packages/shared/**
```
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── src/
    ├── model.ts
    ├── model.test.ts
    ├── git.ts

    ├── shell.test.ts
```

**packages/contracts/**
```
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── src/
    ├── index.ts (barrel export)
    ├── baseSchemas.ts
    ├── model.ts
    ├── orchestration.ts
    ├── git.ts
    ├── ipc.ts
    ├── keybindings.ts
    ├── keybindings.test.ts
    ├── provider.ts
    ├── provider.test.ts
    ├── providerRuntime.ts
    ├── providerRuntime.test.ts
    ├── editor.ts
    ├── project.ts
    ├── server.ts
    ├── terminal.ts
    ├── terminal.test.ts
    ├── ws.ts
    ├── ws.test.ts
    └── orchestration.test.ts
```

---

### 2. SUBPATH EXPORTS CONFIGURATION

**packages/shared/package.json - exports field:**
```json
"exports": {
  "./model": {
    "types": "./src/model.ts",
    "import": "./src/model.ts"
  },
  "./git": {
    "types": "./src/git.ts",
    "import": "./src/git.ts"
  },
  "./logging": {
    "types": "./src/logging.ts",
    "import": "./src/logging.ts"
  },
  "./shell": {
    "types": "./src/shell.ts",
    "import": "./src/shell.ts"
  },
  "./Net": {
    "types": "./src/Net.ts",
    "import": "./src/Net.ts"
  }
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Direct source file exports (no build step, uses TS directly)
- Package is marked as private (`"private": true`)
- Type version matches import version (both point to .ts files)
- Subpath exports allow granular imports: `@t3tools/shared/model`, `@t3tools/shared/git`, etc.

**packages/contracts/package.json - exports field:**
```json
"exports": {
  ".": {
    "types": "./src/index.ts",
    "import": "./src/index.ts",
    "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
  }
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Single main entry point (index.ts barrel export)
- Built distribution files for CJS (`dist/index.cjs`)
- Build step: Uses `tsdown` to generate both ESM and CJS outputs
- `files` field restricts package to `dist` directory for distribution

---

### 3. ALL SOURCE FILES - FULL CONTENTS

**packages/shared/src/model.ts** (79 lines)
Provides model/provider utilities:
- `getModelOptions(provider)` - Returns model options for a provider
- `getDefaultModel(provider)` - Returns default model slug
- `normalizeModelSlug(model, provider)` - Normalizes and validates model slugs with alias support
- `resolveModelSlug(model, provider)` - Resolves model slug with fallback to default
- `resolveModelSlugForProvider(provider, model)` - Provider-scoped resolution
- `getReasoningEffortOptions(provider)` - Returns reasoning effort levels
- `getDefaultReasoningEffort(provider)` - Returns default reasoning effort
- Re-exports `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS` from contracts

Types/data imported from `@t3tools/contracts`:
- `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_OPTIONS`, `DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PROVIDER`, `MODEL_OPTIONS_BY_PROVIDER`, `MODEL_SLUG_ALIASES_BY_PROVIDER`, `CodexReasoningEffort`, `ModelSlug`, `ProviderKind`

**packages/shared/src/git.ts** (62 lines)
Provides git branch sanitization utilities:
- `sanitizeBranchFragment(raw: string): string` - Converts arbitrary strings to valid git branch fragments (lowercase, removes quotes, limits to 64 chars)
- `sanitizeFeatureBranchName(raw: string): string` - Ensures `feature/` prefix structure
- `resolveAutoFeatureBranchName(existingBranchNames, preferredBranch): string` - Generates unique feature branch names with numeric suffixes for collision avoidance

No external dependencies, pure string utilities.

**packages/shared/src/logging.ts** (116 lines)
Provides rotating file sink for logging:
- `RotatingFileSinkOptions` interface:
  - `filePath: string`
  - `maxBytes: number` (file size limit)
  - `maxFiles: number` (number of backup files to keep)
  - `throwOnError?: boolean` (optional error handling flag)
- `RotatingFileSink` class:
  - Constructor validates maxBytes and maxFiles >= 1
  - `write(chunk)` - Appends string or Buffer data, auto-rotates when exceeding maxBytes
  - Private methods: `rotate()`, `pruneOverflowBackups()`, `readCurrentSize()`, `withSuffix()`
  - Rotates files with numbered suffixes (e.g., `log.1`, `log.2`)

Uses Node.js native modules: `fs`, `path`. No external dependencies.

**packages/shared/src/shell.ts** (39 lines)
Provides shell PATH environment extraction:
- `extractPathFromShellOutput(output: string): string | null` - Parses shell output with custom markers to extract PATH value
- `readPathFromLoginShell(shell: string, execFile?): string | undefined` - Executes login shell with `-ilc` flag to extract full PATH from shell startup
- Uses custom markers (`__T3CODE_PATH_START__` and `__T3CODE_PATH_END__`) to isolate PATH output
- 5-second timeout for shell execution
- Supports custom execFile implementation for testing

Uses Node.js native: `child_process.execFileSync`.

**packages/shared/src/Net.ts** (181 lines)
Provides networking utilities using Effect library:
- `NetError` - Tagged error class extending `Data.TaggedError`
- `NetServiceShape` interface with methods:
  - `canListenOnHost(port, host): Effect<boolean>` - Tests TCP binding
  - `isPortAvailableOnLoopback(port): Effect<boolean>` - Checks both IPv4 and IPv6 loopback
  - `reserveLoopbackPort(host?): Effect<number, NetError>` - Reserves ephemeral loopback port
  - `findAvailablePort(preferred): Effect<number, NetError>` - Finds available port, tries preferred first
- `NetService` class - Service tag using Effect's `ServiceMap.Service`
- Implements Effect-based service layer pattern
- Uses Node.js `net` module
- Handles EADDRNOTAVAIL errors gracefully for IPv6-absent hosts

**packages/shared/src/model.test.ts** (70 lines)
Tests for model utilities using Vitest:
- Tests `normalizeModelSlug()` with aliases, empty values, prototype pollution resistance
- Tests `resolveModelSlug()` with defaults, unknown models, supported options
- Tests backward compatibility with codex defaults
- Tests `getReasoningEffortOptions()` returns correct options
- Tests `getDefaultReasoningEffort()` provider-scoped defaults

**packages/shared/src/shell.test.ts** (56 lines)
Tests for shell utilities using Vitest:
- Tests `extractPathFromShellOutput()` extracts between markers, ignores surrounding noise
- Tests `readPathFromLoginShell()` with mocked execFile, validates shell command args (-ilc flag)
- Validates timeout and marker presence in command

**packages/shared/src/Net.test.ts** (99 lines)
Tests for Net service using Effect + Vitest:
- Uses Effect test layer pattern with `it.layer(NetService.layer)`
- Tests `reserveLoopbackPort()` returns positive port
- Tests `isPortAvailableOnLoopback()` detects occupied ports
- Tests `findAvailablePort()` with preferred and fallback scenarios
- Uses `Effect.acquireUseRelease` for resource management in tests

---

### 4. TYPESCRIPT CONFIGURATION

**packages/shared/tsconfig.json:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Simple configuration extending the workspace base.

**packages/contracts/tsconfig.json:**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "plugins": [
      {
        "name": "@effect/language-service",
        "namespaceImportPackages": ["@effect/platform-node"],
        "diagnosticSeverity": {
          "importFromBarrel": "error",
          "anyUnknownInErrorContext": "warning",
          "instanceOfSchema": "warning",
          "deterministicKeys": "warning"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

Includes Effect Language Service plugin for strict type checking with Effect patterns.

**Root tsconfig.base.json (relevant settings):**
- `target: "ES2023"`
- `module: "ESNext"`
- `moduleResolution: "Bundler"`
- `strict: true`
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`
- `noImplicitOverride: true`

---

### 5. TYPES/INTERFACES PATTERNS

**packages/shared patterns:**
1. **No schema validation** - Shared utilities use plain TypeScript types
2. **Function-based exports** - Utility functions with clear I/O types
3. **Service pattern** (Net.ts):
   - Uses Effect library's `ServiceMap.Service` for dependency injection
   - Implements Effect-based async/error handling
4. **Class-based implementations** (RotatingFileSink):
   - Constructor validates configuration
   - Private methods for internal state management
5. **Callback-based async** - Net service uses Effect.callback pattern

Here's a summary of what I found and what the plan covers:

---

## What I Discovered

**Existing patterns to follow:**
- `packages/shared` exports source `.ts` files directly via `package.json` subpath exports — no build step. Adding `./themes` is a one-liner.
- Theme/settings state uses **module-level `useSyncExternalStore`** (no React Context anywhere in the app) — see `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. The new hook mirrors this exactly.
- Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties on `:root`. Setting `document.documentElement.style.setProperty("--background", ...)` at runtime overrides stylesheet declarations with full CSS variable inheritance — `--card`, `--popover`, etc. that derive from `--background` all update automatically.
- The existing `useTheme.ts` manages the `.dark` class (light/dark/system). The new hook will also toggle `.dark` based on the theme's `colorScheme`, and silently sync `t3code:theme` in localStorage so both hooks agree (no feedback loop since `localStorage.setItem` in the same tab doesn't fire `StorageEvent`).

---

## The 6-Step Plan

1. **`packages/shared/src/themes.ts`** — `Theme` interface, `ThemeColors`, three built-in themes (t3-dark, t3-light, t3-midnight), `getTheme(id)` and `listThemes()`. Pure TypeScript, no Effect.
2. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./themes"` subpath export.
3. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add `--font-family` and `--font-size-base` CSS vars to `:root`; wire `body` to use them so the hook can override fonts.
4. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts`** — New hook: reads/writes `t3code:custom-theme`, applies CSS vars + `.dark` class + font on load and on change, exports `useCustomTheme()` with `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes }`.
5. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Grid of 3 theme cards, each with 4 color swatches (background, foreground, accent, sidebar circles) and a name/badge, using existing Tailwind class patterns from the settings radio buttons.
6. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Import `ThemeSelector` and insert a "Color Theme" subsection inside the existing Appearance section, after the "Active theme:" line.

The request is to build a richer, extensible **custom theme system** on top of that: a shared `Theme` type with full color + font definitions, at least three built-in themes, a `useCustomTheme()` hook that persists selection and applies CSS custom properties at runtime, a selector UI with color swatches, and wiring into the existing Settings page. Constraints: - No new dependencies; no build-config changes. - `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` must pass. - Follow existing code patterns exactly (module-level `useSyncExternalStore`, Tailwind v4 CSS vars, subpath exports in `packages/shared`). --- ## Critical Files | File | Role | |---|---| | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `./themes` subpath export | | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **NEW** — Theme type + built-ins + helpers | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Existing dark/light hook — read-only reference | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts` | **NEW** — custom theme hook | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **NEW** — selector UI with swatches | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Add ThemeSelector inside Appearance section | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | Add `--font-family` / `--font-size-base` CSS vars | --- ## Step-by-Step Plan ### Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` (new file) Use plain TypeScript only (no Effect — consistent with `model.ts`, `git.ts`). ```ts export interface ThemeColors { background: string; // maps to CSS --background foreground: string; // maps to CSS --foreground accent: string; // maps to CSS --primary and --ring sidebar: string; // stored as CSS --sidebar (forward-looking) editor: string; // stored as CSS --editor (forward-looking) terminal: string; // stored as CSS --terminal-bg (forward-looking) statusBar: string; // stored as CSS --status-bar (forward-looking) } export interface Theme { id: string; name: string; colors: ThemeColors; fontFamily: string; fontSize: number; // px, e.g. 14 colorScheme: "light" | "dark"; // drives .dark class + color-scheme property } ``` Define three named `const` objects (avoid array-index access under `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`): | id | name | colorScheme | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `t3-dark` | T3 Dark | dark | Matches existing dark mode defaults (`neutral-950` base, blue accent `oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`) | | `t3-light` | T3 Light | light | Matches existing light mode defaults (`#ffffff` base, blue accent `oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`) | | `t3-midnight` | T3 Midnight | dark | GitHub-dark-inspired (`#0d1117` base, `#58a6ff` accent, deeper sidebar `#010409`) | Color values (exact): ``` t3-dark: background: "color-mix(in srgb, #030304 95%, #ffffff)" foreground: "#f3f4f6" accent: "oklch(0.588 0.217 264)" sidebar: "color-mix(in srgb, #030304 95%, #ffffff)" editor: "color-mix(in srgb, #030304 95%, #ffffff)" terminal: "#0e1218" statusBar: "#0a0b0e" t3-light: background: "#ffffff" foreground: "#1f2937" accent: "oklch(0.488 0.217 264)" sidebar: "#ffffff" editor: "#ffffff" terminal: "#ffffff" statusBar: "#f9fafb" t3-midnight: background: "#0d1117" foreground: "#e6edf3" accent: "#58a6ff" sidebar: "#010409" editor: "#0d1117" terminal: "#0d1117" statusBar: "#161b22" ``` Exports: ```ts const BUILT_IN_THEMES: readonly Theme[] = [T3_DARK, T3_LIGHT, T3_MIDNIGHT]; export function listThemes(): readonly Theme[] { return BUILT_IN_THEMES; } // Falls back to T3_DARK (named const, never undefined) if id not found. export function getTheme(id: string): Theme { return BUILT_IN_THEMES.find((t) => t.id === id) ?? T3_DARK; } ``` All three themes share the same `fontFamily` (DM Sans stack, matching `index.css`) and `fontSize: 14`. --- ### Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` Add one entry to the `exports` field (same pattern as existing entries): ```json "./themes": { "types": "./src/themes.ts", "import": "./src/themes.ts" } ``` --- ### Step 3 — `apps/web/src/index.css` Add two CSS custom properties to `:root` and wire the `body` font to use them. This allows the theme hook to override font at runtime without touching the stylesheet again. In `:root` block (before the `@variant dark` block): ```css --font-family: "DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; --font-size-base: 14px; ``` Change `body` font-family declaration: ```css body { font-family: var(--font-family); font-size: var(--font-size-base); ... } ``` --- ### Step 4 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts` (new file) Mirrors the exact pattern of `useTheme.ts` (module-level store + `useSyncExternalStore`). **localStorage key:** `t3code:custom-theme` **Default:** `"t3-dark"` ``` Module-level state: - listeners[] - cachedId: string | null | undefined getStoredId(): string → read localStorage, validate against known ids, default to "t3-dark" getSnapshot(): Theme → getTheme(getStoredId()) — stable ref via getTheme's ?? Toggle .dark class: document.documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", theme.colorScheme === "dark") 3. Set document.documentElement.style.colorScheme = theme.colorScheme 4. On the next React reconcile, `useTheme`'s `getSnapshot` will read the updated value and reflect it correctly. --- ### Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new file) Small presentational component, zero new dependencies. `, after the closing `

` tag of "Active theme: …" (line 241–243 in current file) and before `

Color Theme

Choose a color theme for the editor.

``` No other changes to this file. --- ## Interaction Between `useTheme` and `useCustomTheme` ``` On settings page both hooks are active simultaneously. useTheme manages: t3code:theme key → .dark class (light/dark/system) useCustomTheme manages: t3code:custom-theme key → .dark class + ALL CSS vars On initial load: Both module-level `applyX` calls fire. useCustomTheme.ts is imported second (imported by ThemeSelector which is in a leaf component), so its apply runs after useTheme's. Since CSS vars set via style.setProperty always win over stylesheet declarations, the custom theme values are authoritative. On theme change via ThemeSelector: applyCustomTheme runs → .dark class updated → all CSS vars overridden inline → t3code:theme updated to match → useTheme's getSnapshot returns new value on next render → Appearance section "Active theme:" label updates. On dark-mode toggle (System/Light/Dark radio): useTheme.setTheme runs → .dark class toggled BUT: custom theme CSS vars remain as inline styles, so colors don't change. This is intentional: the dark/light toggle controls system-UI chrome, the custom theme controls component colors. ``` --- ## Verification 1. **Typecheck:** `bun typecheck` in root (covers all packages via project references). 2. **Lint:** `bun lint` in root. 3. **Runtime test:** - Open Settings → Appearance section. - Confirm "Color Theme" subsection shows 3 cards with swatches. - Click "T3 Midnight" → app colors change immediately; verify `document.documentElement` has `.dark` class and inline CSS vars set (`--background: #0d1117`, etc.). - Reload page → Midnight theme persists (localStorage `t3code:custom-theme = "t3-midnight"`). - Click "T3 Light" → `.dark` removed, light colors applied; "Active theme: light" shows. - Click "T3 Dark" (default) → returns to original dark look. 4. **Cross-tab:** Open second tab, change theme → first tab updates (StorageEvent sync).

Here's a summary of what I found and what the plan covers:

---

## What I Discovered

**Existing patterns to follow:**
- `packages/shared` exports source `.ts` files directly via `package.json` subpath exports — no build step. Adding `./themes` is a one-liner.
- Theme/settings state uses **module-level `useSyncExternalStore`** (no React Context anywhere in the app) — see `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. The new hook mirrors this exactly.
- Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties on `:root`. Setting `document.documentElement.style.setProperty("--background", ...)` at runtime overrides stylesheet declarations with full CSS variable inheritance — `--card`, `--popover`, etc. that derive from `--background` all update automatically.
- The existing `useTheme.ts` manages the `.dark` class (light/dark/system). The new hook will also toggle `.dark` based on the theme's `colorScheme`, and silently sync `t3code:theme` in localStorage so both hooks agree (no feedback loop since `localStorage.setItem` in the same tab doesn't fire `StorageEvent`).

---

## The 6-Step Plan

1. **`packages/shared/src/themes.ts`** — `Theme` interface, `ThemeColors`, three built-in themes (t3-dark, t3-light, t3-midnight), `getTheme(id)` and `listThemes()`. Pure TypeScript, no Effect.
2. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Add `"./themes"` subpath export.
3. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Add `--font-family` and `--font-size-base` CSS vars to `:root`; wire `body` to use them so the hook can override fonts.
4. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.ts`** — New hook: reads/writes `t3code:custom-theme`, applies CSS vars + `.dark` class + font on load and on change, exports `useCustomTheme()` with `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes }`.
5. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Grid of 3 theme cards, each with 4 color swatches (background, foreground, accent, sidebar circles) and a name/badge, using existing Tailwind class patterns from the settings radio buttons.
6. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Import `ThemeSelector` and insert a "Color Theme" subsection inside the existing Appearance section, after the "Active theme:" line.

Now I need to implement the actual hook that stores the theme in localStorage and applies these variables plus the dark class toggle on load and whenever the theme changes. I'm realizing there's a conflict here—both the existing `useTheme` hook and my new `useCustomTheme` hook are trying to manage the `.dark` class, which will cause them to fight each other. I need to make sure the new hook also syncs the `t3code:theme` key in localStorage so both hooks stay in agreement about whether the theme is dark or light.

Now I'm examining how the existing code uses `useSyncExternalStore` with a module-level store pattern, following the same approach as `useTheme.ts` and `appSettings.ts`. I'm also looking at how the CSS variables are set up in the existing dark mode styles to understand how the `.dark` class triggers the variant styles.

Still writing the hook implementation... I need to add `colorScheme` to the Theme interface since it's shared logic, then start implementing the themes file with the interface definitions and built-in theme configurations.

Defining theme structures... I'm reconsidering the color values—`color-mix` might not have full browser support in inline styles, so I should switch to simpler hex or oklch values instead. Looking back at the existing dark mode setup, I see it uses Tailwind CSS custom properties, but for the theme definition itself, plain color values would be clearer and more reliable.

Now I'm thinking through how CSS variable inheritance works—if I set `--background` at runtime, the derived variables like `--card` that reference it in the stylesheet should automatically update since they're using `var(--background)`.

So I really only need to set the core variables: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, and `--ring`. The theme-specific ones like `--theme-sidebar` and `--theme-editor` can be custom variables for future use, but the existing CSS will cascade properly from just those four main overrides.

Now I'm working through the actual color values for the dark theme, starting with the background color which is currently a very dark neutral with a slight white tint. I've defined the color palettes for all three themes with specific hex values and oklch colors for the accents, sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar. Now I'm moving on to implement the actual theme configuration, starting with the font settings that will be shared across all variants.

Looking at how the existing `useTheme` hook works, I need to build a new `useCustomTheme` hook that reads the stored custom theme ID, applies CSS variables to the root element, manages the dark class toggle based on the color scheme, and keeps the theme identifier in sync to avoid conflicts with the existing theme system. match the custom theme's colorScheme, `useTheme` will read that value and apply the correct dark/light class without conflicting with the custom theme's styling. in the same tab doesn't fire a `StorageEvent`, `useTheme`'s subscribe handler won't fire and it won't call `emitChange()`. But `useTheme`'s getSnapshot reads from localStorage on each render...

Hmm, but `useSyncExternalStore` only calls `getSnapshot` when subscribers notify. The `useTheme` subscribers won't be notified because we set `t3code:theme` without calling `useTheme`'s `emitChange`. And the `useTheme` hook calls `applyTheme(theme)` in a `useEffect`, which only runs when `theme` (from the snapshot) changes. Since the snapshot didn't change from `useTheme`'s perspective, `applyTheme` won't be called again.

EXCEPT: on initial mount, `useTheme` will call `applyTheme(theme)` regardless. If `useCustomTheme` applies the dark theme first at module load time, then `useTheme`'s `useEffect` runs and could override it.

I think the simplest approach is to have `useCustomTheme` apply the custom theme's colors and also update `t3code:theme` in localStorage so that `useTheme` reads the right value next time. This keeps both hooks in sync.

The tricky part is the initial module load order—since both hooks get imported around the same time, there's no guaranteed execution order.
